data.text: Exclusive-Ukraine images indicate Russia used missile at heart of nuclear pact collapse
data.snippet: Images of debris from Russian strikes on Ukraine strongly indicate that Moscow has used a cruise missile whose development led Donald Trump to quit a landmark nuclear pact in his first term, two experts said, confirming earlier Reuters reporting.

image.text: Purported image of fragments of a Russian 9M729 missile provided by Ukrainian law enforcement sources

data.text: US, Ukraine discuss post-war reconstruction as Russia pummels grid
data.snippet: Ukrainian and U.S. officials met in Geneva on Thursday for talks on post-war reconstruction despite a deadlock in peace negotiations with Russia, which pounded infrastructure across Ukraine with drone and missile strikes overnight.

image.text: Aftermath of a Russian drone and missile attack in Kharkiv

data.text: Denmark to hold elections as PM bets on Greenland crisis boost
data.snippet: Denmark will hold a parliamentary election on March 24, Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said on Thursday, seeking to capitalise on a surge in support for her defiant stance against U.S. pressure over Greenland.

image.text: The Radicals kickstart the election campaign

data.text: Stellantis CEO vows profit rebound after 20 billion euro EV writedown hit
data.snippet: Stellantis CEO Antonio Filosa promised a profitability comeback this year, after the automaker reported on Thursday a massive earnings hit linked to multi-billion euro charges caused by its scaled-back electric-vehicle ambitions.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Stellantis launches production of new hybrid Fiat 500

data.text: ECB's Lagarde again hints at completing term
data.snippet: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde hinted again at serving out her term to complete projects including the digital euro, in a fresh pushback on Thursday against speculation about an early exit.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: ECB President Christine Lagarde addresses the press in Frankfurt

data.text: Macron to outline nuclear vision amid European unease over US alliance
data.snippet: President Emmanuel Macron will update France’s nuclear doctrine on Monday, ruling out shared European control while outlining what Paris can offer allies worried about the reliability of the U.S. nuclear umbrella under President Donald Trump.

image.text: French President Macron at the Elysee Palace in Paris

data.text: UBS executive says one item remains in migration of Credit Suisse clients
data.snippet: UBS has one item remaining in the migration of former Credit Suisse clients onto UBS's system, the Switzerland head of the Zurich-based bank said on Thursday.

image.text: A UBS logo is pictured on the branch of the Swiss bank in Lucerne

data.text: European shares retreat from record high, upbeat corporate earnings offset tech declines
data.snippet: Europe's benchmark index retreated from a record high on Thursday, as healthcare and technology shares weighed, while upbeat corporate earnings from the likes of Schneider and Indra limited the overall declines.

image.text: German share price index DAX graph is pictured at the stock exchange in Frankfurt

data.text: To win Europe's embrace, China needs to uncap the yuan: Mike Dolan
data.snippet: With Washington raising barriers, China's push to shift trade toward Europe is running into one big demand: let the yuan rise against the euro and level the playing field.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz visits China

data.text: EU steel sector pushes to be part of 'Made in Europe' act, seeks narrow scope
data.snippet: Europe's steel industry said on Thursday that EU provisions due to be set out next week that prioritise the use of locally-made materials must include steel, and 'local' should be understood as only close EU neighbours such as Britain and Norway.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Steel worker at furnace of ThyssenKrupp's steel plant in Duisburg

data.text: Explainer-Does Trump's new 'surcharge' make EU worse off than under trade deal?
data.snippet: The EU has demanded that the United States stick to the terms of a trade deal they agreed last year after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's global tariffs and he responded with a new, across-the-board "import surcharge".

data.text: Italy's ruling parties strike deal on electoral law overhaul
data.snippet: Italy's ruling parties have agreed on a sweeping overhaul of the electoral law, two sources said on Thursday, a move opposition parties say is designed to keep Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in power after elections due in 2027.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Italian Parliament commemorates Pope Francis, in Rome

data.text: Setback for Meta as court adviser backs EU regulators in data spat
data.snippet: An adviser to Europe's top court on Thursday sided with EU regulators demanding information regarding two antitrust investigations of Meta Platforms, in a setback for the U.S.-based social media company.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Illustration shows teenagers pose for a photo while holding smartphones in front of a Meta logo

data.text: EU seeks G7 coordination on maritime services ban on Russian seaborne oil
data.snippet: The European Union needs to coordinate its plan to impose a full maritime services ban on Russia's seaborne crude oil exports with other G7 countries before pushing ahead with the measure, the EU's sanctions envoy said on Thursday.

image.text: FILE PHOTO: Rosneft's Russian-flagged crude oil tanker Vladimir Monomakh transits the Bosphorus

data.text: Poll shows main rival of French far-right in presidential election could lose mayor seat
data.snippet: Former French Prime Minister Edouard Philippe could lose his seat as mayor of the port city of Le Havre in municipal elections next month, an opinion poll showed, dealing a potential blow to his presidential ambitions for 2027.

image.text: French President Macron meets members of the political parties at the Elysee Palace, in Paris
